title: Liquid Funding Ltd in Maiden Lane LLC / Bear Stearns Bailout Records

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Liquid Funding Ltd in Maiden Lane LLC / Bear Stearns Bailout Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records related to Liquid Funding, Ltd., a Bermuda-incorporated structured finance entity (IBC RUCEC 29378), including but not limited to:

1. Any records related to Liquid Funding, Ltd. in connection with the Maiden Lane LLC portfolio created in March 2008 to facilitate JPMorgan Chase's acquisition of Bear Stearns.
2. Any records identifying whether Liquid Funding's assets, liabilities, or commercial paper were among those transferred to, purchased by, or guaranteed through Maiden Lane LLC or any other Federal Reserve emergency lending facility (2007-2009).
3. Correspondence, memoranda, or analyses referencing Liquid Funding, Ltd., Liquid Funding Holdings LLC, or Global Securitization Services LLC in connection with Bear Stearns' collapse or the Maiden Lane transaction.
4. Any records of due diligence, governance review, or officer/director background checks conducted on Liquid Funding, Ltd. or its parent entities in connection with the Maiden Lane facility.

To the extent responsive records are held primarily by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York, I request any Board of Governors records reflecting oversight, approval, analysis, or review of such records or of the Maiden Lane facility's asset composition.

Background: Liquid Funding, Ltd. had $6.7 billion in outstanding liabilities (Fitch, 2006) and was 40% owned by Bear Stearns. Its commercial paper was held in retail money market funds. Bear Stearns Bank Plc (Dublin) managed its investments. JPMorgan Chase served as Security Trustee. The entity's rated liabilities were reportedly paid in full on April 18, 2008, approximately one month after the Fed-facilitated Bear Stearns bailout. Jeffrey Epstein served as Director and Chairman of this entity from November 2001 to March 2007. I am seeking to determine whether Federal Reserve emergency facilities backstopped this entity's obligations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: December 2001 Executive Privilege Assertion - Campaign Finance Task Force Memos
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records related to the December 13, 2001 assertion of executive privilege by President Bush regarding DOJ memos subpoenaed by the House Committee on Government Reform (chaired by Rep. Dan Burton), including:

1. All records from the Office of White House Counsel (Alberto Gonzales) regarding the decision to invoke executive privilege
2. All communications between the White House and the Department of Justice regarding the subpoenaed documents
3. Copies of the subpoenaed DOJ memos themselves, if retained in White House files — specifically the memoranda from the Chief of the Campaign Financing Task Force to Attorney General Janet Reno recommending appointment of a Special Counsel, all response memoranda, and all deliberative memoranda from other investigations referenced in the President's December 13, 2001 memorandum to the Attorney General
4. All communications with the House Committee on Government Reform regarding the privilege assertion

For context: The executive privilege assertion — the Bush administration's first — blocked Congressional access to Campaign Finance Task Force memos and deliberative memoranda from other Clinton-era investigations. The President's memorandum to the Attorney General is archived at georgewbush-whitehouse.archives.gov (released December 13, 2001). These records have never been publicly released.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

title: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request – Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-210 – Incident Report – Jose Morales / Christine Holloway – December 2–3, 2019 – 6½ Myrtle Avenue, Ansonia, CT

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request – Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-210 – Incident Report – Jose Morales / Christine Holloway – December 2–3, 2019 – 6½ Myrtle Avenue, Ansonia, CT
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting a copy of the incident/offense report for the homicide investigation of Christine Holloway, initiated on December 2–3, 2019, at 6½ Myrtle Avenue, Ansonia, CT.

Victim: Christine Holloway (age 43)
Suspect: Jose Morales (age 48, of New Haven, CT)
Incident: On December 2, 2019, the Ansonia Police Department conducted welfare checks at 6½ Myrtle Avenue after Holloway failed to report to work. Officers forced entry at approximately 7:30 PM and discovered Holloway deceased in the bathtub, having died of blunt force trauma. Holloway's 14-month-old daughter, Vanessa Morales, was not found at the scene. Morales was initially arrested on December 3, 2019, on weapons charges. He was subsequently charged with murder and tampering with evidence on February 7, 2020.
Case status: Fully adjudicated. Morales was convicted of murder and tampering with evidence on April 22, 2025, following a jury trial at Milford Superior Court, and sentenced to 65 years in prison on July 14, 2025.

Please include all narrative portions, supplemental reports, and officer observations associated with this incident covering the period of December 2–3, 2019. I request electronic delivery where possible. Please provide a written fee estimate before processing. If any portion is denied, please identify the specific exemption under the Connecticut FOIA authorizing the withholding and release all segregable non-exempt records.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Johnson

title: SLP PD Flock Safety Audit

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: SLP PD Flock Safety Audit
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) ALPR Audit Trail Logs (All Access & Activity)
Records comprising the ALPR data audit trail documenting all queries, searches, alerts, access, sharing, or dissemination events from 1/1/2025 through the date this request is processed. Per department policy, all such actions must be recorded in a data audit trail. Please include all available metadata, including but not limited to:
- Date and time
- User identifier (name or role)
- Query/search parameters
- Action taken (search, alert review, data access, sharing, etc.)

Please provide these records in a structured format (e.g., CSV, XLSX, or similar database format).
------
2) Access Justification / Purpose Logs
Records documenting the purpose, justification, or case association for each ALPR query or access event during the same time period. Department policy restricts access to legitimate law enforcement purposes tied to investigations or official actions. 
------
3) External Access / Inter-Agency Query Logs
Records of any access to SLP PD ALPR data by external agencies, including:
- Requesting agency
- Requestor identity (or role)
- Date/time
- Stated purpose
- Associated case number (if available)

Department policy requires written requests and documented justification for such access.
[Note - Common Flock usage logs may group 1, 2, and 3 together into one excel file. That is hopefully less work for you, and easier for us.] 
------
4) Manual Hot List Entries and Justifications
Records of all Manual Hot List entries during the requested time period, including:
- Plate entered
- Date of entry/removal
- Justification or associated investigation

Policy requires a “legitimate and specific law enforcement reason” for such entries.
------
5) Agreements with Private Entities (Flock / ALPR Access)
Any agreements, memoranda, or contracts with:

- Homeowner associations
- Private businesses
- Individuals
- Religious entities or groups

Relating to ALPR or Flock system deployment, access, or data sharing.
------
6) Electronic Communications with Flock Safety
Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) between SLP PD personnel and Flock Safety (domain: @flocksafety.com). Timeframe: 6/1/2025 to present
You may de-duplicate mass emails or newsletters.
------
7) Biennial ALPR Audit Reports and Submissions
Copies of all biennial ALPR audit reports, including any reports, summaries, or submissions provided to the Minnesota Commissioner of Administration, legislative committees, or other oversight bodies, for the past two years.
------

I realize this is a large request and appreciate you taking the time to complete it. Please feel free to respond in multiple parts as documents become available.

I have already reviewed: 
A)  Flock Transparency Page: https://transparency.flocksafety.com/saint-louis-park-mn-pd
B) SLP PD ALPR Policy: https://www.stlouisparkmn.gov/home/showpublisheddocument/28047/638681458304130000
C) Location of Devices: https://dps.mn.gov/divisions/bca/data-and-reports/agencies-use-lprs-lpr

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

title: Robert Mueller

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Robert Mueller
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into former FBI Director Robert Swan Mueller III (August 7, 1944 – March 20, 2026). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/21/us/politics/robert-s-mueller-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an assistant editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti
